mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in payroll employment growth above 200k per month signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS regimes fall below 0.5% in > 40% of cases, or the 5-day return correlation with PAYEMS drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in both housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in combined HOUST/RSAFS decline periods exceed -1.5% in < 30% of cases, or the interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of aggressive rate hikes, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high CPI regimes exceed 1.0% in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns becomes positive (r > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals robust manufacturing activity, driving corporate profit growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high INDPRO regimes fall below 0.3% in > 35% of cases, or the INDPRO coefficient in regression analysis is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 2% (GDPC1) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in combined GDPC1/UNRATE decline periods exceed -2.0% in < 25% of cases, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and UNRATE shows no significance (p > 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% creates labor market tightness, pushing wage growth and inflation expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in low UNRATE regimes exceed 1.2% in > 45% of cases, or the UNRATE coefficient in regression analysis is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 104 combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates supply-side bottlenecks, creating inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO/HOUST divergent regimes exceed -0.8% in < 30% of cases, or the interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows no significance (p > 0.15).
